commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a media-only source-suppression bypass for trusted local voice/TTS media in message-tool-only dispatch paths, strips ordinary text from bypass delivery, adds focused regression tests, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection and the linked live Discord/Telegram report: current main stages trusted TTS media but suppresses block/final source delivery in message-tool-only mode before provider send. I did not run a live current-main transport repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review should hold merge until live transport proof or maintainer-run Mantis/Crabbox evidence covers the channel media path; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.

target.title: fix(tts): deliver trusted voice media in message-tool rooms
target.body:
## Summary

- Mark trusted local voice tool media with a media-only source-suppression bypass so dynamic TTS audio can still be delivered in `message_tool_only` channel contexts.
- Honor that bypass for block replies under the same send-policy and room-event guards already used by final replies.
- Strip ordinary assistant text from media-only bypass delivery so "sent it" confirmations stay private while the staged voice media reaches the channel.

Fixes #83636.

## Root cause

Dynamic TTS staged trusted local media, but the pending media was emitted through block/final source-reply paths. In Discord/Telegram-style `message_tool_only` contexts those paths are suppressed unless internal metadata explicitly allows delivery, so the provider send path was never reached.

## Real behavior proof

- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts` (7 tests passed)
- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts` (58 tests passed)
- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts` (54 tests passed)
- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/auto-reply/reply-payload.ts src/auto-reply/types.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

I also attempted the broader requested suites:

- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` failed during import before executing tests because this local dependency tree is missing `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`.
- The combined lifecycle/tool-media acceptance run executed the tool-media tests but `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts` failed during import before executing lifecycle tests because this local dependency tree is missing `@earendil-works/pi-ai`.

I do not have live Discord or Telegram credentials in this environment, so I could not run the optional real-channel smoke.

## Attribution

Please preserve commit author attribution when squashing/reworking, or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`
